description_long: This hotel is a two-story structure with a rustic brick exterior and a prominent, reddish-brown tiled hip roof. The facade features multiple rectangular windows with wooden shutters, and a large wooden double door serves as the main entrance. A notable architectural element is the covered balcony supported by two thick columns, adding depth and character to the front elevation. The building's overall form is L-shaped, with the rooflines intersecting at different angles, creating visual interest. The materials and design evoke a traditional, perhaps Mediterranean or rural European style, with an emphasis on natural textures and earthy tones. The absence of modern signage or glass elements gives the hotel a historic, timeless appearance.
description: A two-story brick hotel with a tiled hip roof, wooden shutters, and a column-supported balcony, evoking a rustic, traditional style.
description_view_0: This view shows the side and partial front of the hotel, highlighting the intersecting rooflines and several shuttered windows.
description_view_1: The front view displays the main entrance, the balcony with two supporting columns, and the symmetrical arrangement of windows.
description_view_2: This angle emphasizes the balcony and columns, with a clear view of the front facade and the arrangement of windows and doors.
description_view_3: The opposite side view reveals more of the brickwork, additional windows, and the main entrance, with the balcony partially visible.
